title: Defining the niche from user input
use_when: Defining the niche from user input. Use when the user needs a product workflow for business analysis related to defining the niche from user input. Trigger terms: pm, business-analysis, positioning, segmentation, niche.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Defining the niche from user input when the request is mainly a different lifecycle artifact; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Defining the niche from user input venture brief with target segment, wedge, assumptions, and validation path
framework_fit: Defining the niche from user input fits Venture / 0-1 work in the Strategize lifecycle when the user needs Defining the niche from user input venture brief with target segment, wedge, assumptions, and validation path and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Defining the niche from user input venture brief with target segment, wedge, assumptions, and validation path without tying recommendations to the user's Strategize stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Defining the niche from user input as generic Venture / 0-1 advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Defining the niche from user input when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $defining-the-niche-from-user-input to turn this defining context into a decision-ready Defining the niche from user input venture brief with target segment, wedge, assumptions, and validation path.
